strengths: 20 years of hands-on software engineering across web, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, exceeding the 15-year minimum
Founder experience (GISCollective, 2016–present) plus current AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work at re:cinq shows builder fluency and entrepreneurial drive
Consistent knowledge-sharing track record — conference talks (FOSS4G 2019), multi-day workshops, and courses — aligns with the 'inspire others' responsibility
concerns: No evidence of director-level or principal-scope influence; career has been senior/staff IC delivery roles, not organization-wide technical strategy
No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, and AI exposure is recent (2025+) tooling work rather than recognized expertise or shipped AI products at scale
Cloud experience (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployments) is practitioner-level, not the cloud platform strategy depth the role centers on

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise and AI product track record
No demonstrated director-level strategic or advisory scope
Experience tenure and entrepreneurial builder profile

response.justification: The candidate clears the minimum bar: a technical bachelor's degree and roughly two decades of software engineering, including performance-critical systems at Dunnhumby (millions of requests/second) and platform founding at GISCollective. However, the role's core competencies are AI strategy leadership, recognized AI expertise, and advising strategic customers at director level. The résumé shows only recent, tooling-level AI work (MCP code generators at re:cinq since April 2025), no AI-related advanced degree, no shipped AI products or platforms of note, and no evidence of setting technical direction beyond team scope. Public speaking and workshop history partially supports the influence dimension, but the gap between senior IC delivery and principal-level AI strategy is substantial.
